posts.text:
We wrote 25 articles covering every feature change from Maximo 7.6 to MAS 9.

Not marketing summaries. Not release notes. A technical breakdown of what changed, what broke, what got better, and what your team needs to know before, during, and after the upgrade.

Here is what the series covers:

-> Platform & Core (Parts 1-8): Architecture shift, Carbon UI, Work Centers removed, dashboards, mobile, security, integration, scripts
-> Suite AI Applications (Parts 9-15): Health scoring, Monitor IoT, Predict ML, Visual Inspection, AI Assist, Optimizer, AppPoints strategy
-> Add-Ons & Industry (Parts 16-20): Licensing changes, MRO Optimization, HSE, Spatial, Service Provider, Industry Solutions
-> Supply Chain (Parts 21-25): Core inventory, procurement, mobile apps, AI pipeline, MRO competitors

Every article includes what changed from 7.6, what your team needs to learn, and the honest gaps IBM does not highlight in the sales pitch.

Bookmark this. Share it with your upgrade team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #AssetManagement #TheMaximoGuys #EAM
posts.comment:
Full series index with reading paths: https://themaximoguys.ai/blog/mas-features-series-index

This is the most comprehensive public breakdown of MAS 9 features available. 25 parts, written by practitioners, not marketing.

@IBM @IBM Maximo Application Suite

Which part of your MAS 9 upgrade is keeping your team up at night?

#DigitalTransformation #CMMS #CloudMigration

posts.text:
MAS 9 is not a version upgrade. It is a re-architecture.

If someone on your team said "we just upgrade the EAR file and redeploy," this is for them.

Here is what changed under the hood:

-> Monolithic WebSphere EAR replaced by containerized microservices on Red Hat OpenShift
-> Java 17 is mandatory and will break most custom Java code
-> The database is sealed in managed deployments -- no direct SQL access
-> Infrastructure teams need Kubernetes, OpenShift, and container networking skills
-> Core business objects, Application Designer, and Automation Scripts remain unchanged

The business logic survived. The entire infrastructure did not.

Your team needs to understand this before they plan a single sprint.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #OpenShift #TheMaximoGuys #CloudNative
posts.comment:

#Kubernetes #Java17 #AssetManagement

posts.text:
Your users will not recognize Maximo when they log into MAS 9.

This is not a skin change. IBM replaced the entire UI framework with Carbon Design System.

-> All legacy skins are gone -- no tivoli09, no tivoli13, no choices
-> Field labels are permanently above fields -- the vertical toggle is gone
-> CSS class names changed from .bx-- to .cds-- between MAS 9.0 and 9.1
-> Custom CSS is unsupported by IBM -- if it breaks, you are on your own
-> Every screenshot in your SOPs and training materials is now wrong

The user training gap is massive. Budget for comprehensive retraining, not a 2-hour overview. Teams report 60-70% productivity in week one, with 3-4 months to full comfort.

This is not optional preparation. This is the cost of the upgrade.

#IBMMaximo #CarbonDesign #MAS9 #TheMaximoGuys #UX

posts.text:
Work Centers are gone. Not hidden. Not renamed. Gone.

This is the single most impactful change for daily Maximo users in MAS 9.

-> Work Centers are completely removed -- replaced by Role-Based Applications built on React.js/MAF
-> There is no automatic migration tooling -- every customization must be manually rebuilt
-> Critical gaps remain: no Purchasing RBAs, no Asset moves/swaps, no Supervisor SR-to-WO conversion
-> Technology changed from XML/JSP to React.js -- existing customizations cannot be ported
-> Budget 3+ weeks per complex Work Center reconstruction

If your team customized Work Centers -- and most teams did -- this post is the most important article in the series. Read it before you plan your migration timeline.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #Migration #TheMaximoGuys #EAM

posts.text:
Your morning ritual just changed. Start Centers have a strategic replacement -- three of them, actually.

-> Operational Dashboard replaces Start Centers with card-based analytics -- but cannot yet be personalized per user or security group
-> Work Queue Manager replaces Result Set Portlets with configurable priority queues visible by person group
-> Maximo Scheduler is now FREE -- all 8 scheduling applications included in base Manage
-> MAS 9.1 adds Planning Dashboard, new optimization algorithms, and KPI Manager with external API support
-> Maximo Optimizer (separate AppPoints) adds automated constraint-based scheduling and route optimization

The Scheduler change alone saves organizations tens of thousands in licensing. If you were paying separately for Maximo Scheduler in 7.6, that cost is gone.

posts.text:
Maximo Anywhere is dead. Your field teams will thank you.

No more MobileFirst crashes. No more Dojo rendering errors. No more device-side builds.

-> Maximo Mobile is built on React.js with Progressive Web App deployment -- no app store required
-> Server-side configuration replaces device-side builds -- updates apply instantly to all devices
-> 7 mobile apps: Technician, Asset Manager, Inspections, Issues/Transfers, Receiving, Inventory Count, Service Request
-> Full offline sync with optimized reconnection -- technicians complete work without connectivity
-> MAS 9.1 adds self-assign from work queue, unassign/reassign, and asset work history in the field

Inspection forms download once at login instead of per-inspection. That alone is a massive performance improvement.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

posts.text:
Three tickets on Monday morning. Three different subsystems. One root cause.

MAS 9 fundamentally rewrote authentication, API security, and mapping.

-> Google Maps and Bing Maps are deprecated -- OpenMap is the new default, ArcGIS for enterprise GIS
-> Authentication now requires an external IdP -- SAML, OIDC, LDAP, or MongoDB local
-> Suite-level SSO is built-in -- no more manual SSO configuration per application
-> API Keys replace basic auth for integrations -- with scope control and automatic rotation
-> MAS 9.1 adds multiple IdP fallback, self-registration, and multiple LDAP server support

If your migration planning has not accounted for these changes, this is your wake-up call.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #CyberSecurity #TheMaximoGuys #GIS

posts.text:
Every BIRT report must be recreated from scratch. There is no migration tool.

That is the reporting story of MAS 9. The integration story is significantly better.

-> JSON API (/api) with lean=1 is the primary integration mechanism -- replaces /maxrest/rest and OSLC
-> RMI is deprecated -- rewrite all existing RMI integrations to REST API
-> Kafka replaces JMS for event-driven integration -- native OpenShift support via Strimzi
-> BIRT is being phased out for Cognos Analytics -- every report recreated manually
-> Simple BIRT reports can often be replaced by Operational Dashboard KPI cards instead of Cognos

The integration architecture is modern and powerful. The reporting migration is painful and unavoidable. Plan for both.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #API #TheMaximoGuys #Kafka

posts.text:
Ninety percent of scripts worked fine. The ten percent that did not were the ten percent that touched every transaction.

MAS 9 keeps Automation Scripts but Java 17 changes the rules.

-> Automation Scripts survive but Java 17 module restrictions require testing ALL scripts in a sandbox
-> IBM recommends converting all custom Java to Automation Scripts (Jython/JavaScript)
-> Reliability Strategies Library is FREE -- 800+ equipment types and 58,000+ failure modes ready to generate PMs
-> AI-Assisted Strategy Building in MAS 9.1 uses AI to suggest failure mechanisms and boundary conditions
-> Manage 9.1 adds natural language queries, field value recommendations, and similar record detection

The Reliability Strategies Library alone justifies a day of exploration. It is the most underused free feature in MAS 9.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #AutomationScripts #TheMaximoGuys #ReliabilityEngineering

posts.text:
The most expensive misunderstanding in every MAS 9 migration: treating it as a Maximo upgrade.

MAS 9 is not Manage with a new interface. It is an Application Suite with 8+ applications.

-> MAS 9 is an Application Suite -- not just Manage with a new UI
-> Manage is the hub -- all other applications read from and write to Manage
-> The integrated pipeline (Sensors -> Monitor -> Health -> Predict -> Manage -> Optimizer) creates multiplicative value
-> Organizations that deploy Manage in isolation miss 80% of what they paid for
-> AppPoints licensing lets you flexibly allocate across any application in the suite

Individual app value is dwarfed by integrated value. The pipeline from sensor data to optimized work orders is where the ROI lives.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #IoT #TheMaximoGuys #PredictiveMaintenance

posts.text:
Your VP asks which of 14,000 assets to replace with a $5M budget. Your reliability engineer opens a spreadsheet with 47 tabs.

Maximo Health replaces that spreadsheet with a score from 0 to 100 on every asset.

-> Health scores combine age, meters, work history, sensors, and inspections into a single condition indicator
-> Asset Investment Optimization answers the budget question: what to maintain, repair, and replace
-> Risk Matrix plots assets on probability vs consequence -- instantly prioritizes your portfolio
-> Data quality is non-negotiable: 90%+ install dates, 2+ years WO history, consistent failure codes
-> Pilot effort: 42-84 hours with 2-3 people -- the fastest suite application to deploy

Health is the first suite application every organization should turn on. The data requirements are achievable, the time to value is weeks, and the ROI is immediate.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

posts.text:
Pump P-2047 tried to warn you for three weeks. Nobody was listening.

Vibration crept up 128%. Bearing temperature ran 12 degrees hot. Flow dropped 8%. Six weeks later, catastrophic failure. $59,000 in unplanned costs.

Maximo Monitor gives your assets a voice.

-> Ingests sensor data via MQTT, REST, CSV, Edge Data Collector, and Kafka Connect
-> Three dashboard types: Summary (fleet), Entity (single device), Custom (user-designed)
-> Anomaly detection includes threshold, statistical, spectral analysis, and custom Python functions
-> Alerts auto-generate work orders, service requests, emails, webhooks, and Kafka events
-> Edge Data Collector enables monitoring at remote sites with store-and-forward for intermittent connectivity

The data was there. The warning was there. Monitor makes sure someone is always listening.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #IoT #TheMaximoGuys #ConditionMonitoring

posts.text:
Every vendor promises predictive maintenance. Maximo Predict actually requires you to earn it.

This is the most data-demanding, skill-intensive application in the entire MAS suite.

-> Four model types: failure probability, predicted failure date, remaining useful life, anomaly detection
-> Data requirements are strict: 20+ failures per mode, 2+ years history, 6+ months sensor data
-> Data science skills ARE required -- you cannot just click Train like Visual Inspection
-> watsonx.ai provides pre-built Jupyter notebooks for common scenarios (pumps, motors, transformers)
-> The chicken-and-egg problem: organizations with great PM programs may lack the failure data needed to train models

The organizations that succeed with Predict walk in with realistic expectations about data, skills, and timeline. The ones that fail buy the license and discover they have seven failures in five years.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #MachineLearning #TheMaximoGuys #PredictiveMaintenance

posts.text:
Human visual inspection is inconsistent. Inspector A finds three defects Monday morning. Inspector B finds five on the same asset Friday afternoon. Neither is wrong.

Maximo Visual Inspection fixes that without requiring a data scientist.

-> Five capabilities: image classification, object detection, anomaly detection, action detection (video), and OCR
-> No data science required -- drag-drop images, assign labels, click Train
-> Edge deployment on NVIDIA Jetson enables real-time inspection without cloud connectivity
-> MAS 9.1 Large Vision Models reduce training data needs for civil infrastructure
-> Manufacturing use cases: weld defects, surface quality, assembly verification, paint quality

Fatigue degrades accuracy after two hours. Bias creeps in after a hundred identical welds. MVI does not get tired, and it does not get bored.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #ComputerVision #TheMaximoGuys #AI

posts.text:
Two fundamental maintenance challenges. Two purpose-built applications.

Getting the right information to the right person. Getting the right person to the right place.

-> AI Assist enables natural language queries, work order creation from plain English, and field recommendations via watsonx.ai
-> MAS 9.1 adds field value recommendations with confidence scores and similar record detection
-> Optimizer solves NP-hard scheduling problems with constraint programming -- 20-30% travel time reduction typical
-> Civil Infrastructure provides regulatory-compliant bridge, road, and tunnel inspection with Large Vision Models
-> Parts Identifier uses computer vision to identify unknown parts from photos

You can say "The HVAC unit is making noise and not cooling, fix today" and AI Assist will draft a work order with the appropriate asset, priority, failure class, and problem code.

posts.text:
Two questions nobody answers honestly:

How much does this cost per user? How long does it actually take?

-> AppPoints are a flexible shared pool -- reallocate as you deploy new applications
-> Role-based allocation optimizes spending: Technician at 10 points vs Maintenance Manager at 30
-> The 13-month roadmap is realistic -- 6-month timelines usually fail because they skip data quality
-> Phase 0 (data quality) determines everything -- fix install dates, failure codes, and asset classification first
-> Success criteria per phase prevent premature advancement to the next phase

We have seen organizations buy thousands of AppPoints, allocate them on day one across every application, and wonder six months later why Health scores are meaningless and Predict models will not train.

The problem was never the technology. The problem was sequencing and data quality.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

posts.topic: Licensing: Free vs Paid in MAS 9
posts.text:
Your organization may be paying for Maximo modules that are now included for free.

IBM restructured MAS 9 licensing. Five previously paid modules are now in base Manage.

-> FREE: Calibration, Linear Assets, Maximo Mobile, Scheduler (8 apps), Reliability Strategies (800+ asset types, 58K failure modes)
-> STILL PAID: HSE, Spatial, Service Provider, ACM, and industry solutions require separate licensing or Premium-tier AppPoints
-> MRO Inventory Optimization is a new cloud SaaS product starting at $3,094/month
-> AppPoints optimization comes from mixing user tiers strategically -- not everyone needs Premium
-> Organizations upgrading from 7.6 should audit their license agreements immediately

If you are still paying separately for Calibration, Linear Assets, Scheduler, or Mobile from your 7.6 contract, those costs should be removed from your renewal.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #Licensing #TheMaximoGuys #CostOptimization

posts.text:
Two paid add-ons that change how you manage parts and safety. Neither existed in Maximo 7.6 in this form.

-> MRO Inventory Optimization uses AI to calculate ROP/MAX and typically delivers 20-40% inventory reduction
-> Essentials starts at $3,094/month for up to $50M inventory value; Standard adds demand forecasting and what-if analysis
-> MRO IO integrates with Manage, SAP, and Oracle via API -- it is not Maximo-exclusive
-> HSE Manager contains 50+ applications across 6 modules: Incident, Permit to Work, Emissions, Operations, MOC, Competency
-> MAS 9 HSE adds Envizi ESG integration for Scope 1-2-3 greenhouse gas reporting

MRO IO is a cloud SaaS product that reads your inventory data and pushes optimized values back. HSE covers the full safety and environmental lifecycle.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

posts.topic: Spatial, Service Provider, ACM & Maximo IT
posts.text:
Four paid add-ons that rarely show up in the standard sales pitch. For the organizations that need them, they are non-negotiable.

-> Maximo Spatial integrates with Esri ArcGIS for enterprise GIS -- Bing and Google Maps are deprecated
-> Service Provider enables multi-tenant outsourced maintenance from a single Manage instance with SLA tracking and billing
-> ACM (Complex Assets) tracks as-designed vs. as-built vs. as-maintained configurations for regulated, serialized assets
-> Maximo IT brings ITSM into MAS -- service desk, CMDB, change management -- unifying OT and IT
-> All four require additional AppPoints beyond base Manage and specific prerequisites

These are not nice-to-haves. They are the difference between Maximo being a good work management system and being the system of record for your entire operational model.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #GIS #TheMaximoGuys #ITSM

posts.text:
Three products that signal where IBM is betting the future of MAS. None existed in Maximo 7.6.

-> Maximo Renewables (Prescinto acquisition) has three modules: Monitor, Analyze, and Drone Thermography for wind, solar, and battery storage
-> TRIRIGA becomes Maximo Real Estate & Facilities Management in MAS 9.1 with eight modules covering space, leases, sustainability, and workplace experience
-> The connector ecosystem includes SAP, Oracle, Workday, Envizi, watsonx.ai, and Cloud Pak for Data
-> Renewables AI models are pre-trained for renewable energy -- something IBM did not have internally
-> Each connector is separate -- deploy only what you need

Organizations running wind, solar, or battery storage assets should evaluate Renewables immediately. The Prescinto AI models are purpose-built for renewable energy performance.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #Renewables #TheMaximoGuys #TRIRIGA

posts.text:
Six industries. Six regulatory frameworks. One platform.

MAS 9 Industry Solutions extend Manage with deep compliance capabilities.

-> Aviation: MSG-3 programs, Airworthiness Directives, serialized parts traceability under FAA/EASA/ICAO
-> Transportation: Fleet lifecycle, linear assets, mileage-based PMs under FTA/FRA/DOT
-> Utilities: Grid management, outage response, smart meters under NERC/FERC/EPA
-> Oil & Gas: Process safety, pipeline management, turnarounds under OSHA PSM/API
-> Nuclear: Tech spec LCO tracking, surveillance testing under NRC/INPO/ASME -- the most complex implementation
-> Civil Infrastructure: NBI bridge inspection with Large Vision Models under FHWA/NBIS/MAP-21

All require Premium-tier AppPoints (15 per concurrent user). Civil Infrastructure got the biggest MAS 9 upgrade.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

#IBMMaximo #MAS9 #RegulatoryCompliance #TheMaximoGuys #IndustrySolutions

posts.text:
Your storeroom processes have not changed since 7.5. MAS 9 adds features on top of everything you already missed.

-> Supply chain features organized in three tiers: Manage license (core), AppPoints (AI suite), and paid SaaS (MRO IO)
-> Inventory module tracks balances across unlimited storerooms with eight transaction types and barcode integration
-> Six item types cover every material category: Standard, Rotating, Condition-Enabled, Lotted, Service, and Kit
-> Transfer lifecycle follows a four-stage workflow: ENTERED, STAGED, SHIPPED, COMPLETE with full shipment management
-> Count books support six selection methods, blind counting, trial reconciliation, and barcode-driven mobile counting

This is the foundation that Parts 22-25 build on. Every supply chain capability in MAS 9 starts here.

posts.text:
Ten contract types. Five cost accounting methods. Zero Purchasing RBAs.

MAS 9 procurement is comprehensive and powerful -- but the modernization is incomplete.

-> Procurement flows from Desktop Requisitions through PRs to POs, with RFQ-based multi-vendor comparison and change orders
-> Ten contract types: Purchase, Blanket, Pricing, Labor, Lease, Rental, Service, Warranty, Software, and Master
-> Receiving supports PO-based receipt, inspection, rotating asset receipt, and MAS 9.1 return processing
-> Five cost methods (Average, Last, FIFO, LIFO, Standard), dynamic lead time calculation, automated reorder processing
-> Critical gap: there is NO Purchasing RBA yet -- procurement teams must use classic Maximo applications

The procurement backbone is mature. The RBA modernization for purchasing is the biggest remaining gap in MAS 9 supply chain.

Full article in comments. Save this for your team.

posts.text:
Four dedicated mobile supply chain apps replace everything Maximo Anywhere offered -- and then some.

-> Issues and Transfers Mobile: staging, barcode scanning, storeroom-to-storeroom transfers, tools transfer, full offline
-> Inventory Count Mobile: barcode-driven counting, blind count mode, photo attachments, approval workflows -- but reconciliation is online only
-> Receiving Mobile: PO-based receipts, rotating asset receipt, inspection on receipt -- MAS 9.1 adds bin updates and return processing
-> Technician App: field material requests, usage recording, barcode scanning, planned material views, availability checks
-> Migration from Anywhere eliminates device-side builds entirely -- server-side configuration only

No more MAF rebuilds. No more manual APK distribution. No more XML customization. Server-side config, instant updates, every device.

posts.text:
Six MAS suite applications form a complete AI-powered supply chain pipeline. Together they transform parts management from reactive to predictive.

-> AI Assist: intelligent search, WO material recommendations, failure-to-parts mapping, document summarization
-> Parts Identifier: photograph unknown parts in the field, get instant catalog matches with stock levels
-> Health: scores drive demand predictability, trigger advance replacement procurement, enable criticality-based stocking
-> Predict: tells you WHEN failure occurs -- enabling pre-positioned parts and elimination of emergency procurement
-> Monitor: sensor data triggers condition-based orders, tracks consumable usage rates, refines reorder points
-> Optimizer: schedules work only when both materials and labor are available -- reducing waste and idle inventory

From sensor reading to parts reservation without human intervention. That is the pipeline.

81% of MRO order quantities are wrong based on manual calculations. That is the problem. Here is the landscape.

This is the final installment of our 25-part MAS Features series.

-> IBM MRO IO uses AI with 23 optimization capabilities starting at $37K/year -- the lowest barrier to entry in the market
-> Seven competitors profiled: Syncron (best aftermarket ML), Baxter (financial TCO), Servigistics (deepest multi-echelon), IFS, Infor, Verusen (works with dirty data), Kinaxis
-> IBM's key weakness: no multi-echelon optimization -- if you have a complex distribution network, Servigistics or Syncron fills that gap
-> Verusen is the only vendor that requires zero data cleansing -- $20M capital unlocked and 10x ROI
-> The 12-month, 5-phase roadmap covers 425 hours across 14 topic areas from Foundation through Advanced rollout

25 parts. Every change from Maximo 7.6 to MAS 9. Written by practitioners, not marketing.
